items.title: Gradient descent update rule
items.description: Batch gradient descent uses η in θ ← θ − η∇L; the learning rate is the scalar that turns a gradient direction into a step size.

items.title: Stochastic gradient descent
items.description: SGD applies the same η-scaled update with noisy mini-batch gradients; η must balance speed against gradient variance.

items.title: Loss surface geometry
items.description: Curvature and valley width on the loss surface determine how large η can be before overshoot; steep or ill-conditioned regions need smaller effective steps.

items.title: Deep learning frameworks
items.description: Framework optimizers and training loops expose η as `lr`, update it via schedulers each epoch or step, and log it for debugging.
